normalization_point: Silicon2-style K-beauty distribution can be Stage 2/3, but sell-through, reorder, inventory, receivables, and OPM decide Green.
D'Alba/VT/Clio-style brands can be Stage 2/3, but IPO doubles and single-brand rerating need 4B-watch.
CJ/Olive Young is Stage 2 platform optionality; CJ Stage 3 requires cash-flow or NAV discount transmission.
Cosmax/Kolmar/C&C-style OEM/ODM can be early Stage 3 candidates when multi-brand repeat orders convert into OPM/FCF.
Nongshim-style global staple brand is Stage 2/3 when overseas sales and mainstream shelf gains convert into OP/EPS.
Single-SKU K-food can route research but is hard-capped before portfolio repeat demand and OP/EPS evidence.
F&F-style licensed brands are Watch/Red when China exposure, logo fatigue, and M&A governance risk dominate.
Amorepacific/LG H&H-style China premium exposure is a hard 4C watch inside K-beauty.
Tariff can cap K-beauty/K-food Stage 3 unless margin buffer and price pass-through are proven.
Shipment growth is capped before sell-through, reorder, inventory days, receivables days, and margin quality are verified.
Consumer/brand headlines cannot support Stage 3-Green until channel, reorder, inventory, and margin details are verified.
Dr.G-style M&A validates K-beauty strategic value, but listed-company Stage 3 still needs direct revenue/OPM link.
A strong private platform cannot create listed-parent Green without cash-flow, IPO, or NAV-discount transmission.
